---
title: "Bellows Falls Union High School PCB Summary"
author: 
  - "Principal Investigator: Keri Hornbuckle Ph.D."
  - "Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering and IIHR - Hydroscience and Engineering"
  - "Email: keri-hornbuckle@uiowa.edu"
  - "Phone: (319) 384-0789"
subtitle: Prepared by the University of Iowa for the Vermont Department of Environmental
  Conservation
date: "Revised `r format(Sys.Date(), '%B %d, %Y')`"
output:
  pdf_document: 
    fig_width: 7
    fig_height: 4
  word_document: default
header-includes:
  - \usepackage{placeins}
---


------------------------------------------------------------------------

------------------------------------------------------------------------

```{r libraries, include=FALSE}

rm(list=ls(all=TRUE))
options(tinytex.verbose=TRUE)
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)
library(scales)
library(dplyr)
library(ggpubr)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(ztable)
library(tinytex)
library(kableExtra)
library(knitr)
library(png)
library(docxtools)
library(lsa)
#knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

```

```{r names, include=FALSE}

# school name

sch.name <- 'Bellows Falls Union High School'
city <- 'Westminster'

# school abbreviation

sch.ab <- 'BF'

# school specific parameters

t.days <- 26               # deployment time in days
temp.c = 24                # average temp in Celsius 


```

```{r readIn, include=FALSE}


#######################################################
# READ IN DATA FILES AND FIXED FOR USE
#######################################################
# CHANGE AS NEEDED

cong.list <- read.csv('//kchResearch.drive.engineering.uiowa.edu/kchresearch/Jason Hua/Vermont Schools Project/Data/bellowsFalls/bf_ssMass_20240124.csv', header = FALSE)

mass <- read.csv('//kchResearch.drive.engineering.uiowa.edu/kchresearch/Jason Hua/Vermont Schools Project/Data/bellowsFalls/bf_ssMass_20240124.csv',
                 header = TRUE, fileEncoding="UTF-8-BOM", check.names = FALSE)
sch.ssMass <- read.csv('//kchResearch.drive.engineering.uiowa.edu/kchresearch/Jason Hua/Vermont Schools Project/Data/bellowsFalls/bf_ssMass_20240124.csv',
                      header = TRUE, fileEncoding="UTF-8-BOM", check.names = FALSE)  # ss corrected mass, back up data frame
samp.type <- read.csv('//kchResearch.drive.engineering.uiowa.edu/kchresearch/Jason Hua/Vermont Schools Project/Data/bellowsFalls/bfSampleType_20240124.csv',
                      header = TRUE, fileEncoding="UTF-8-BOM", check.names = FALSE)   # sample type and materials
samp.type2 <- read.csv('//kchResearch.drive.engineering.uiowa.edu/kchresearch/Jason Hua/Vermont Schools Project/Data/bellowsFalls/bfSampleType_20240124.csv',
                      header = TRUE, fileEncoding="UTF-8-BOM", check.names = FALSE)   # sample type and materials
sch.blanks <- read.csv('//kchResearch.drive.engineering.uiowa.edu/kchresearch/Jason Hua/Vermont Schools Project/Data/bellowsFalls/bf_blanks_20240124.csv',
                      header = TRUE, fileEncoding="UTF-8-BOM", check.names = FALSE)    # SS corrected mass
sch.uncMass <- read.csv('//kchResearch.drive.engineering.uiowa.edu/kchresearch/Jason Hua/Vermont Schools Project/Data/bellowsFalls/bf_uncMass_20240124.csv',
                       header = TRUE, fileEncoding="UTF-8-BOM", check.names = FALSE) # uncorrected mass

ss.rcv <- read.csv('//kchResearch.drive.engineering.uiowa.edu/kchresearch/Jason Hua/Vermont Schools Project/Data/bellowsFalls/bf_ssRecoveries_20240124.csv',
                      header = TRUE, fileEncoding="UTF-8-BOM", check.names = FALSE)   #surrogate recoveries as decimals
# srm <- read.csv('Z:/Jason Hua/Vermont Schools Project/Data/srm_20220817.csv',
#                       header = TRUE, fileEncoding="UTF-8-BOM", check.names = FALSE)

pcb <- read.csv("//kchResearch.drive.engineering.uiowa.edu/kchresearch/Jason Hua/Vermont Schools Project/Data/koaCalc.csv", header = TRUE,
                fileEncoding="UTF-8-BOM", check.names = FALSE)   # pcb koa values/info for calculation
# pcb info: congener, orthos, MW, RRT, koa @ 25C
# columns MUST be in this order for script to work
aroclor <- read.csv('//kchResearch.drive.engineering.uiowa.edu/kchresearch/Jason Hua/Vermont Schools Project/Data/aroclorProfiles_20221025.csv',
                    header = TRUE, fileEncoding="UTF-8-BOM", check.names = FALSE)
aroclor.v <- read.csv('//kchResearch.drive.engineering.uiowa.edu/kchresearch/Jason Hua/Vermont Schools Project/Data/aroclorVapProfiles_20221025.csv',
                    header = TRUE, fileEncoding="UTF-8-BOM", check.names = FALSE)


```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

# FIXED DATA FILES FOR USE
#######################################################

sid <- mass[,2]
names(sid) <- 'sid'
bid <- mass[,1]
cong.list <- cong.list[1,]
cong.list <- cong.list[,-(1:2)]
cong.list <- as.data.frame(t(cong.list))
names(cong.list) <- 'Congener'
samp.list <- mass[,1:2] 
mass <- mass[,-(1:2)] 
# sch.uncMass <- sch.uncMass[,-(1:2)]
materials <- samp.type[,5]
bid.list <- as.data.frame(t(bid))
names(bid) <- 'bid'
samp.mass <- mass[1:(nrow(mass)),]        ######### CHANGE FOR EACH SCHOOL
ae.samp <- samp.list[1:(nrow(mass)),]
clist <- cong.list
names(clist) <- 'congener'
title <- samp.type[,1]


```


```{r functions, include=FALSE}

# FUNCTION USED IN THIS SCRIPT
#######################################################


# mass fraction
##################################################

mass.fraction <- function(sums, cong.list){
r <- nrow(sums)
c <- ncol(sums)
sums.t <- as.data.frame(rowSums(sums))
mass.f <- as.data.frame(matrix(nrow = r, ncol = c))
for (i in 1:r) {
for (j in 1:c) {
mass.f[i,j] <- sums[i,j] / sums.t[i,]
}
}
colnames(mass.f) <- t(cong.list)
#print(rowSums(mass.f))
return(mass.f)
}



# multiple profile plots w/ top ten labels
##################################################

# plots profile
# read data in as .csv with heading = TRUE

profile.plots <- function(data, top, title){
  
#setwd("Z:/Jason Hua/Vermont Schools Project/School Data/Oak Grove Brattleboro school/Results/Labeled Profiles")   #SET DIRECTORY: WHERE FIGURES WILL BE SAVED
data[,1] <- factor(data[,1], level = data[,1])                                           #CHANGE DIRECTORY IF NEEDED
  
for (i in 2:ncol(data)) {

  gtmp <- as.data.frame(top[,i-1])
  names(gtmp) <- 'congener'
  dat.temp <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = nrow(data), ncol = 2))
  mf.tmp <- data[,i]
  names(mf.tmp) <- 'mass.fraction'
  dat.temp <- cbind(clist, mf.tmp)
  dat.temp[,1] <- factor(dat.temp[,1], level = dat.temp[,1]) 
  lab.idx <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = nrow(gtmp), ncol = ncol(gtmp)))
  names(lab.idx) <- 'index'
  lab.idx$index <- 'TRUE'
  lab.idx <- cbind(gtmp, lab.idx)
  data.merge <- merge(x = dat.temp, y = lab.idx, by = 'congener', all = TRUE)
  
  temp.plot <- ggplot(data = data.merge, aes(x = congener, y = mf.tmp)) +    #graph
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = .3, position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  geom_text(data = subset(data.merge, index %in% 'TRUE'), aes(label = congener),
  size = 4.7, vjust = -0.6, check_overlap = TRUE, angle = 45, family = 'serif') +
  ggtitle(title[i-1]) +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = expansion(add=c(2,2))) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0), limits = c(0,30)) +
  xlab(expression(bold("Congener"))) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 11, family = 'serif')) +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 2/5) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
  axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
  axis.line.x = element_blank(),
  axis.line.y = element_blank()) +
  theme(panel.border = element_rect(color =  'black', fill = NA, size = 1.1)) +
  ylab(expression(bold("Mass Fraction (%)")))
  ggsave(paste(title[i-1],".png",sep=""),temp.plot)
  print(temp.plot)
}
}


# profile plot w/ top ten labels
##################################################

# plots one profile
# read data in as .csv with heading = TRUE

profile.plot <- function(data, top, congener.list, title){

  # clr <- "#2C4E74"
data[,1] <- factor(data[,1], level = data[,1])                                           
  names(congener.list) <- 'congener'
  gtmp <- as.data.frame(top)
  names(gtmp) <- 'congener'
  dat.temp <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = nrow(data), ncol = 2))
  mf.tmp <- data[,2]
  names(mf.tmp) <- 'mass.fraction'
  dat.temp <- cbind(congener.list, mf.tmp)
  dat.temp[,1] <- factor(dat.temp[,1], level = dat.temp[,1]) 
  lab.idx <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = nrow(gtmp), ncol = ncol(gtmp)))
  names(lab.idx) <- 'index'
  lab.idx$index <- 'TRUE'
  lab.idx <- cbind(gtmp, lab.idx)
  data.merge <- merge(x = dat.temp, y = lab.idx, by = 'congener', all = TRUE)
  
  temp.plot <- ggplot(data = data.merge, aes(x = congener, y = mf.tmp)) +    #graph
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = .5, position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  geom_text(data = subset(data.merge, index %in% 'TRUE'), aes(label = congener), colour='black',
  size = 3.65,  hjust = -0.3, check_overlap = TRUE, angle = 90, family = 'serif') +
  ggtitle(title) +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = expansion(add=c(2,2))) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0), limits = c(0,25)) +
  xlab(expression(bold("Congener"))) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 14, family = 'serif')) +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 3/7) +
  theme(plot.title=element_text(hjust=0.05,vjust=3,size=11,margin = margin(t=40,b=-25)),
  axis.text.x = element_blank(),
  legend.position = "none",
  axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
  axis.line.x = element_blank(),
  axis.line.y = element_blank()) +
  theme(panel.border = element_rect(color =  'black', fill = NA, size = 1.1)) +
  ylab(expression(bold("Mass Fraction (%)")))

return(temp.plot)
}


# profile plot w/ top ten labels and error bars
##################################################

# plots one profile
# read data in as .csv with heading = TRUE

average.plot <- function(data, top, congener.list, title, sd){

  # clr <- "#2C4E74"
data[,1] <- factor(data[,1], level = data[,1])                                           
  names(congener.list) <- 'congener'
  gtmp <- as.data.frame(top)
  names(gtmp) <- 'congener'
  dat.temp <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = nrow(data), ncol = 2))
  mf.tmp <- data[,2]
  names(mf.tmp) <- 'mass.fraction'
  dat.temp <- cbind(congener.list, mf.tmp)
  dat.temp[,1] <- factor(dat.temp[,1], level = dat.temp[,1]) 
  lab.idx <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = nrow(gtmp), ncol = ncol(gtmp)))
  names(lab.idx) <- 'index'
  lab.idx$index <- 'TRUE'
  lab.idx <- cbind(gtmp, lab.idx)
  data.merge <- merge(x = dat.temp, y = lab.idx, by = 'congener', all = TRUE)
  
  temp.plot <- ggplot(data = data.merge, aes(x = congener, y = mf.tmp)) +    #graph
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width =0.5, position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=mf.tmp-sd,ymax=mf.tmp+sd),width=2,colour="black",size=0.15)+
  geom_text(data = subset(data.merge, index %in% 'TRUE'), aes(label = congener), colour="black",
  size = 3.65,  hjust = -0.6, check_overlap = TRUE, angle = 90, family = 'serif',position=position_dodge(0.9)) +
  ggtitle(title) +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = expansion(add=c(2,2))) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0), limits = c(0,30)) +
  xlab(expression(bold("Congener"))) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 14, family = 'serif')) +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 3/7) +
  theme(plot.title=element_text(hjust=0.05,vjust=3,size=11,margin = margin(t=40,b=-25)),
  axis.text.x = element_blank(),
  legend.position = "none",
  axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
  axis.line.x = element_blank(),
  axis.line.y = element_blank()) +
  theme(panel.border = element_rect(color =  'black', fill = NA, size = 1.1)) +
  ylab(expression(bold("Mass Fraction (%)")))

return(temp.plot)
}


# profile plots no labels
##################################################

# plots profile
# read data in as .csv with heading = TRUE

plain.plot <- function(data, title){

# setwd("Z:/Jason Hua/Vermont Schools Project/School Data/Oak Grove Brattleboro school/Results/Unlabeled Profiles")   #SET DIRECTORY: WHERE FIGURES WILL BE SAVED
data[,1] <- factor(data[,1], level = data[,1])                                           #CHANGE DIRECTORY IF NEEDED

  for (i in 2:ncol(data)) {
    temp.plot <-  ggplot(data = data, aes(x = data[,1], y = data[,i])) +    # profile graph
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = .5, position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
    ggtitle(title[i-1]) +
    scale_x_discrete(expand = expansion(add=c(1,1))) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0), limits = c(0,35)) +
    xlab(expression(bold("Congener"))) +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(text = element_text(size = 14, family = 'serif')) +
    theme(aspect.ratio = 2/5) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x = element_blank(),
    axis.line.y = element_blank()) +
    theme(panel.border = element_rect(color =  'black', fill = NA, size = 1.1)) +
    ylab(expression(bold("Mass Fraction (%)")))
    # ggsave(paste(title[i-1],".png",sep=""),temp.plot)
    print(temp.plot)
  }
}


# kpuf calculations
##################################################
kpuf.calc <- function(pcb, R, temp.c) {
  duoa <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = nrow(pcb), ncol = 1))
  koa <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = nrow(pcb), ncol = 1))
    for (i in 1:nrow(pcb)) {
      duoa[i,1] <- (-0.13 * pcb[i,3] + 2.9 * pcb[i,2] - 47.8) * 1000
    }
    for (j in 1:nrow(pcb)) {
      koa[j,1] <- pcb[j,5] - (duoa[j,1] / (2.303 * R)) * ((1 / (273.15 + temp.c)) - (1 / 298.15))
    }
  l.kpuf <- (0.6366 * koa) - 3.1774
  kpuf <- 10^(l.kpuf)
  return(kpuf)
}

```

\newpage

# Executive Summary

The purpose of this document is to present results of PCB analyses of `r sch.name`, `r city`, Vermont. 

The University of Iowa team, in collaboration with Vermont and school officials, deployed 19 polyurethane foam passive air samplers (PUF-PAS), 26 polyurethane foam passive emission samplers (PUF-PES), and 7 field blanks across 6 rooms in `r sch.name` (`r sch.ab`) for `r t.days` days, from October 21, 2023, to November 17, 2023 (Table 1).     

Methods used for this study are research methods previously reported (Hua et al., 2023; Bannavti, Jahnke et al., 2021; Herkert et al 2018). Briefly, samples, field blanks, and laboratory blanks were extracted with acetone and hexane, purified and concentrated, and then analyzed using a gas chromatograph with triple quadrupole mass spectrometry. The method produces mass (ng) values for all 209 possible PCB congeners minus one congener (PCB 204) used for quality control purposes. Some PCBs cannot be individually separated, and the method reports congener results as 173 congeners or coeluting congeners. Quality assurance and control protocols address accuracy, precision, representativeness, reproducibility and comparability.  


```{r execSum, echo=FALSE}


#samp.type <- samp.type[order(samp.type$room,samp.type$sample.type),]

tbl1.1 <- samp.type2[samp.type2$materials != 'Blank',]
tbl1.2 <- samp.type2[samp.type2$materials == 'Blank',]

knitr::kable(tbl1.1, row.names=F,caption = 'Summary of samples analyzed.',col.names = c('Batch (BID)','Sample ID (SID)','Sample Type', 'Room Number','Material Sampled'), align = 'ccccc', booktabs=TRUE) %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = "HOLD_position")
knitr::kable(tbl1.2, row.names=F,caption = 'Summary of blank samples analyzed.',col.names = c('Batch (BID)','Sample ID (SID)','Sample Type', 'Room Number','Material Sampled'), align = 'ccccc', booktabs=TRUE) %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = "HOLD_position")
```

## Sampler Deployment Locations 

![](//kchResearch.drive.engineering.uiowa.edu/kchresearch/Jason Hua/Vermont Schools Project/School Data/Bellows Falls/Figures and Images/bfSampleMap.png)

\newpage

# Results

```{r echo=FALSE}
##############################################################################

# EFFECTIVE VOLUME, VEFF

##############################################################################
# VEFF ASSUMPTIONS AND CONSTANTS - CHANGE AS NEEDED
##################################################


ws.on <- 0.11   #m/s
ws.off <- 0.07  #m/s
f.on <- 0.36
f.off <- 1 - f.on
c <- 1.326
puf.sa = 0.0153 #m2
v.puf <- 2.295e-04 #m3 
v.puf.t <- as.character(v.puf)
den.puf <- 21300 #g/m3 

R = 8.3144               # gas constant
cong <- pcb[,1]
names(cong) <- 'Congener'
###################################################

v.const <- as.character(rbind(t.days,ws.on,ws.off,f.on,f.off,temp.c,R,c,puf.sa,v.puf.t))
const.name <- c('Deployment Time (days)', 'Windspeed, Vent On (m s\\textsuperscript{-1})', 'Windspeed, Vent Off (m s\\textsuperscript{-1})', 'Fraction Vent On','Fraction Vent Off','Room Temperature (\\textsuperscript{o}C)',
                'Gas Constant (J K\\textsuperscript{-1} mol\\textsuperscript{-1})','Sampler Constant',
                'PUF Surface Area (m\\textsuperscript{2})','PUF Volume (m\\textsuperscript{3})')
const.name <- as.data.frame(const.name)
vari.v <- c('d','WS\\textsubscript{on}','WS\\textsubscript{off}','f\\textsubscript{on}','f\\textsubscript{off}','T','R','c','SA','V\\textsubscript{PUF}')
v.const.t <- cbind(const.name,vari.v,v.const)


```

## Effective Air Sampling Volume for PUF-PAS

The effective sampling volume ($V_{eff}$, m^3^) is the congener specific volume of air passing through the PUF-PAS during the deployment time (Table 2). The effective volume is calculated as a function of the partitioning coefficient ($K_{PUF},\text{ } unitless$), the deployment time ($d,\text{ } days$), the PUF volume ($V_{PUF},\text{ } m^3$), and the sampling rate ($R_{s},\text{ } m^3 d^{-1}$) (Herkert and Hornbuckle, 2018). The indoor $R_{S}$ is a function of the fraction of the day when the ventilation is on or off ($f_{on}/f_{off},\text{ } unitless$), the windspeed when the ventilation is on/off ($WS_{on}/WS_{off},\text{ } m s^{-1}$), the molecular weight of each congener ($MW,\text{ } g\text{ } mol^{-1}$), the air temperature ($T,\text{ } ^{o}C$), and the empirical constant of the air samplers ($c,\text{ } unitless$). Effective air sampling volumes are provided in Table 4.

Five temperature sensors were deployed in five rooms (auditorium, girlsLocker, lockerOffice, dWing, utility). Temperature profiles in all rooms were similar throughout the deployment period. Therefore, one temperature (`r temp.c` C) was used for calculating the effective volume. Average temperatures were calculated for each temperature sensor. Then the averages were averaged again to get the final value. 

```{r echo=FALSE}

knitr::kable(v.const.t, format.args= list(big.mark=','), row.names = FALSE, col.names = c('','',''), caption='Assumptions used for calculating effective volume and sampling rate.', escape=F, digits = , valign='t', align='lcr', booktabs=TRUE) %>% 
  kable_styling(latex_options = "HOLD_position") %>%
  column_spec(1,width='2.5in') %>%
  column_spec(2,width = '1.5in') %>%
  column_spec(3,width = '1.5in')


```

### Sampling Rate

<!-- The air temperature of the room during sampler deployment was approximately 22.8$^{o}C$ (73$^{o}F$). We assumed that the air temperature of the room was constant throughout the deployment time.  -->
$$ R_{s} = (f_{on}\sqrt{WS_{on}}+f_{off}\sqrt{WS_{off}})({1\over \root 3\of{MW}})10^{[0.0012T+c]}$$

### Effective Volume

$$V_{eff} = (V_{PUF}K_{PUF})[1-e^{-R_{S}t\over V_{PUF}K_{PUF}}]$$

```{r veff, echo=FALSE}

##############################################################################

# EFFECTIVE VOLUME, VEFF

##############################################################################
# koa to kpuf based on temperature

kpuf <- kpuf.calc(pcb,R,temp.c)
kpuf <- kpuf * den.puf
##################################################
# Rs and Veff calculations

rs <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = nrow(pcb), ncol = 1))

for (l in 1:nrow(pcb)) {
  
rs[l,1] <- ((f.on * (sqrt(ws.on))) + (f.off * (sqrt(ws.off))))*(1/(pcb[l,3]^(1/3)))*(10^((0.0012*t.days)+c))

}

v.eff <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = nrow(pcb), ncol = 1))
for (ii in 1:nrow(pcb)) {
v.eff[ii,1] <- (v.puf * kpuf[ii,1]) * (1 - (exp((-(rs[ii,1] / (v.puf * kpuf[ii,1]))* t.days))))
}
names(v.eff) <- 'V_eff'
V.eff <- v.eff[-c(13,28,29,30,33,47,53,62,65,69,70,71,74,75,76,97,100,101,109,113,116,119,124,125,138,140,149,151,157,163,166,168,173,193,199,204),]
V.eff <- cbind(cong.list,V.eff)

v.eff1 <- V.eff[1:44,]
v.eff2 <- V.eff[45:88,]
v.eff3 <- V.eff[89:132,]
v.eff4 <- V.eff[133:176,]
v.eff.t <- cbind(v.eff1,v.eff2,v.eff3,v.eff4)
options(knitr.kable.NA='')
knitr::kable(v.eff.t, row.names = FALSE, caption='Calculated effective volume (V$_{eff}$, m$^{3}$) for each congener.', digits = 2, valign='t', align='cc', booktabs=TRUE) %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = "HOLD_position")

```

## Sample Summary

Concentrations and emissions of airborne PCBs are determined for each congener or coeluting congener group. Table 5 provides the sum of the congener air concentrations $(ng \text{ } m^{-3})$ or emissions $(ng \text{ } m^{-2} \text{ } day^{-1})$. Individual congener air concentrations and emissions are provided in a separate database. Air concentrations are calculated as the sum of each congener's mass divided by the respective $V_{eff}$ (Table 4). Emissions are calculated as the total mass divided by the PUF surface area (0.0153 m^2^) divided by the deployment time (`r t.days` days). More details about blanks are provided in the "Laboratory Blanks and Field Blanks" section. Air samples and emission samples were well above the values measured in the blanks. 

```{r school, echo=FALSE}


# mass fraction calculation
mass.frac <- mass.fraction(mass,cong.list)
mass.frac <- mass.frac*100
mass.frac <- as.data.frame(t(mass.frac))
mass.frac <- cbind(cong.list, mass.frac)

# air concentration calculation
sch.mass.idx <- sch.ssMass
sch.air.t <- merge.data.frame(sch.mass.idx, samp.type)
sch.air <- sch.air.t[sch.air.t$sample.type == 'Air',1:176]
sch.air.list <- as.data.frame(sch.air[,1])
names(sch.air.list) <- 'bid'
sch.air <- sch.air[,-(c(1,2,176))]

sch.air.lb <- sch.air.t[sch.air.t$sample.type == 'Lab Blank',1:176]
sch.air.llist <- as.data.frame(sch.air.lb[,1])
names(sch.air.llist) <- 'bid'
sch.air.lb <- sch.air.lb[,-(c(1,2,176))]

sch.air.fb <- sch.air.t[sch.air.t$sample.type == 'Field Blank',1:176]
sch.air.flist <- as.data.frame(sch.air.fb[,1])
names(sch.air.flist) <- 'bid'
sch.air.fb <- sch.air.fb[,-(c(1,2,176))] 

conc.calc <- function(fsch.air,veff,flist){
  
sch.conc <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = nrow(fsch.air), ncol = ncol(fsch.air)))
for (c.i in 1:nrow(fsch.air)) {
  for (c.j in  1:ncol(fsch.air)) {
    sch.conc[c.i,c.j] <- fsch.air[c.i,c.j] / veff[c.j,2]
  }
}

sch.conc.t <- as.data.frame(rowSums(sch.conc))
sch.conc.t <- signif(sch.conc.t,2)        # significant figures
names(sch.conc.t) <- 'Concentration'
sch.conc.t <- cbind(flist, sch.conc.t)   # final concentrations ng/m3

return(sch.conc.t)
}

sch.air.conc <- conc.calc(sch.air,V.eff,sch.air.list)
sch.lb.conc <- conc.calc(sch.air.lb,V.eff,sch.air.llist)
sch.fb.conc <- conc.calc(sch.air.fb,V.eff,sch.air.flist)

conc.sum <- rbind(sch.air.conc,sch.lb.conc,sch.fb.conc)

sum.conc <- merge(samp.type,conc.sum,all=TRUE)

##########################################
# emissions calculation
##########################################
# sample emissions
sch.em <- merge.data.frame(sch.mass.idx, samp.type)
sch.em.s <- sch.em[sch.em$sample.type == 'Emissions',1:176]
sch.em.list <- as.data.frame(sch.em.s[,1])
names(sch.em.list) <- 'bid'
sch.em.s <- sch.em.s[,-(c(1,2,176))]

sch.em.t <- as.data.frame(rowSums(sch.em.s))
sch.em.t <- sch.em.t / puf.sa  # ng/m2
sch.em.d <- sch.em.t / t.days  # ng/m2/d
sch.em.d <- signif(sch.em.d,2)       # significant figures
names(sch.em.d) <- 'Emissions'
sch.em.final <- cbind(sch.em.list, sch.em.d)    # final emissions ng/m2/d

# lab blank emissions
sch.em.l <- sch.em[sch.em$sample.type == 'Lab Blank',1:176]
sch.em.llist <- as.data.frame(sch.em.l[,1])
names(sch.em.llist) <- 'bid'
sch.em.l <- sch.em.l[,-(c(1,2,176))]

sch.em.tl <- as.data.frame(rowSums(sch.em.l))
sch.em.tl <- sch.em.tl / puf.sa  # ng/m2
sch.em.dl <- sch.em.tl / t.days  # ng/m2/d
sch.em.dl <- signif(sch.em.dl,1)       # significant figures
names(sch.em.dl) <- 'Emissions'
sch.em.lf <- cbind(sch.em.llist, sch.em.dl)    # final emissions ng/m2/d

# field blank emissions
sch.em.f <- sch.em[sch.em$sample.type == 'Field Blank',1:176]
sch.em.flist <- as.data.frame(sch.em.f[,1])
names(sch.em.flist) <- 'bid'
sch.em.f <- sch.em.f[,-(c(1,2,176))]

sch.em.tf <- as.data.frame(rowSums(sch.em.f))
sch.em.tf <- sch.em.tf / puf.sa  # ng/m2
sch.em.df <- sch.em.tf / t.days  # ng/m2/d
sch.em.df <- signif(sch.em.df,1)       # significant figures
names(sch.em.df) <- 'Emissions'
sch.em.ff <- cbind(sch.em.flist, sch.em.df)    # final emissions ng/m2/d

# negative control emissions
# sch.em.nc <- sch.em[sch.em$sample.type == 'Negative Control',1:176]
# sch.em.nclist <- as.data.frame(sch.em.nc[,1])
# names(sch.em.nclist) <- 'bid'
# sch.em.nc <- sch.em.nc[,-(c(1,2,176))]
# 
# sch.em.tn <- as.data.frame(rowSums(sch.em.nc))
# sch.em.tn <- sch.em.tn / puf.sa  # ng/m2
# sch.em.dn <- sch.em.tn / t.days  # ng/m2/d
# sch.em.dn <- signif(sch.em.dn,1)       # significant figures
# names(sch.em.dn) <- 'Emissions'
# sch.em.ncf <- cbind(sch.em.nclist, sch.em.dn)    # final emissions ng/m2/d

#,sch.em.ncf

sum.em <- rbind(sch.em.final,sch.em.lf,sch.em.ff)

summary.f <- merge(sum.conc,sum.em,all=TRUE)

mat.list <- summary.f[,5]
bl.sum <- summary.f[summary.f$materials == 'Blank',]
bl.id <- bl.sum[,1]
sb.order <- sch.blanks[order(sch.blanks[,1]),]
bl.tot <- rowSums(sb.order[,-1])
names(bl.tot) <- 'Total Mass'
bl.sum <- cbind(bl.sum,bl.tot)
summary <- summary.f[,-c(1,2,3,5)]
summary <- cbind(mat.list,summary)

#########

# summary.f: full summary with IDs

full.summ <- summary
blank.sum <- bl.sum[,c(4,3,6,7)]

summary <- summary[summary$mat.list != 'Blank',] 
summary <- summary[,c(2,1,3,4)]
summary <- summary[order(summary$room,-(summary$Concentration),-(summary$Emissions)),]  # sort by __
f.summary <- full.summ[order(full.summ$room,-(full.summ$Concentration),-(full.summ$Emissions)),]
```

```{r, echo=F}

# conc.cong <- function(fsch.air,veff,flist){
# 
# sch.conc <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = nrow(fsch.air), ncol = ncol(fsch.air)))
# for (c.i in 1:nrow(fsch.air)) {
#   for (c.j in  1:ncol(fsch.air)) {
#     sch.conc[c.i,c.j] <- fsch.air[c.i,c.j] / veff[c.j,2]
#   }
# }
# 
# return(sch.conc)
# }
# air.conc.cong <- conc.cong(sch.air,V.eff,sch.air.list)
# air.conc.cong <- cbind(sch.air.list,air.conc.cong)
# blks <- rbind(sch.air.lb,sch.air.fb)
# blk.list <- rbind(sch.air.llist,sch.air.flist)
# bl.conc.cong <- conc.cong(blks,V.eff,sch.air.list)
# bl.conc.cong <- cbind(blk.list,bl.conc.cong)
# final.conc <- rbind(air.conc.cong,bl.conc.cong)
# write.csv(final.conc,"//kchResearch.drive.engineering.uiowa.edu/kchresearch/Jason Hua/Vermont Schools Project/Results and Reports/Bellows Falls/Final Data/bf_concentrations.csv",row.names=F)
# 
# s.em.cong <- sch.em.s / puf.sa / t.days
# s.em.cong <- cbind(sch.em.list,s.em.cong)
# l.em.cong <- sch.em.l / puf.sa / t.days
# l.em.cong <- cbind(sch.em.llist,l.em.cong)
# f.em.cong <- sch.em.f / puf.sa / t.days
# f.em.cong <- cbind(sch.em.flist,f.em.cong)
# final.em <- rbind(s.em.cong,l.em.cong,f.em.cong)
# write.csv(final.em,"//kchResearch.drive.engineering.uiowa.edu/kchresearch/Jason Hua/Vermont Schools Project/Results and Reports/Bellows Falls/Final Data/bf_emissions.csv",row.names=F)


```

```{r tables, echo=FALSE}
knitr::kable(summary,caption='Summary of air concentrations (ng m$^{-3}$) and emissions (ng m$^{-2}$ d$^{-1}$) of materials.',
             format.args= list(big.mark=','), row.names=FALSE,
             col.names = c('Room Number','Material','Air Concentration','Emissions'),align='c',
             digits = 2, booktabs=TRUE) %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = 'HOLD_position') %>%
  column_spec(1,width = '1in') %>%
  column_spec(2,width = '2in') %>%
  column_spec(3,width = '1in') %>%
  column_spec(4,width = '1in') 

# knitr::kable(blank.sum,caption='Summary of blank measurements as air concentrations (ng m$^{-3}$) and emissions (ng m$^{-2}$ d$^{-1}$).',
#              format.args= list(big.mark=','), row.names=FALSE,
#              col.names = c('Room Number','Material','Air Concentration','Emissions'),align='c',
#              digits = 2, booktabs=TRUE) %>%
#   kable_styling(latex_options = 'HOLD_position') %>%
#   column_spec(1,width = '1in') %>%
#   column_spec(2,width = '2in') %>%
#   column_spec(3,width = '1in') %>%
#   column_spec(4,width = '1in')

```

```{r fullSummary, echo=FALSE}
# fig.show='hide'
# full table with IDs:
# 
id.sum <- summary.f[summary.f$materials != 'Blank',]
id.sum <- id.sum[order(id.sum$room,-(id.sum$Concentration),-(id.sum$Emissions)),]  # sort by __

knitr::kable(id.sum,caption='Summary of air concentrations (ng m$^{-3}$) and emissions (ng m$^{-2}$ d$^{-1}$) of materials.',
             format.args= list(big.mark=','), row.names=FALSE,
             col.names = c('BID','SID','Sample Type','Room Number','Material','Air Concentration','Emissions'),align='c',
             digits = 2, booktabs=TRUE) %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = 'HOLD_position') %>%
  column_spec(1,width = '.3in') %>%
  column_spec(2,width = '.3in') %>%
  column_spec(3,width = '.7in') %>%
  column_spec(4,width = '.7in')  %>%
  column_spec(5,width = '.7in') %>%
  column_spec(6,width = '.7in') %>%
  column_spec(7,width = '.7in')


```

\newpage
\FloatBarrier
# Quality Assurance and Quality Control

Quality assurance and control is evaluated for accuracy, precision, representativeness, reproducibility, and comparability to other methods (Tables 6, 7, 8, 9, 10).  Laboratory blanks (cleaned and stored PUF) and field blanks (cleaned PUF sealed and stored with deployed samples) are included to determine of limits of quantification (LOQ) for each congener.  Because the LOQ masses (ng) are very low, no LOQ subtraction is performed. For direct comparison with samples, LOQ values are reported as air concentrations by dividing the LOQ mass  by the average congener $V_{eff}$ in this study, and as emissions by dividing the LOQ mass by the surface area and deployment time of emission samples used at this school.  Isotopically labeled PCB congeners are injected in each sample and blank to quantify the extraction efficiency.  Congener masses measured in samples and blanks are corrected for surrogate recovery. Standard Reference Material from the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST SRM 2585 - Organic Contaminants in House Dust) was added to clean PUF and analyzed following the same methods for PCBs in PUF.  Results of this analysis has been previously reported and confirms the accuracy of the methods (Hua et al., Suppl. S1, 2023).

## Laboratory Blanks and Field Blanks

Laboratory blanks were extracted with each batch of samples following the same treatment and extraction procedure. Field blanks were transported with samples following the same process except for deployment in a sampler. Each set of field blanks were kept on location with the samplers until the deployment period was over. All blanks were shipped back to the University of Iowa and extracted for PCBs following the same procedure as deployed samples. Table 6 reports the mass, concentrations, and emissions for each blank sample. Concentrations were calculated as mass divided by effective volume, using the same $V_{eff}$ in Table 4. Emissions were calculated as mass divided by the PUF surface area divided by the deployment time. 

```{r blanks, echo=FALSE}
bl.sum2 <- bl.sum[,c(1,3,4,8,6,7)]

knitr::kable(bl.sum2,caption='Summary of blank samples reported as mass (ng), air concentrations (ng m$^{-3}$), and emissions (ng m$^{-2}$ d$^{-1}$).', row.names=FALSE,
             col.names = c('BID','Sample Type','Room Number','Total Mass','Concentration','Emissions'),align='c',
             digits = 2, booktabs=TRUE) %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = 'HOLD_position')
```

## Limit of Quantification (LOQ)

The limit of quantification (LOQ) was calculated as the upper limit of the 99th confidence interval of the log 10-transformed blank masses: average congener mass in blank PUF samples plus 2.325 times the standard deviation divided by the square root of n. Tables with LOQ reported as mass, equivalent concentrations, and equivalent emissions are included in the supplementary folder. The LOQ as concentration was calculated as mass divided by effective volume, using the same $V_{eff}$ in Table 4. The LOQ as emission is calculated as mass divided by the PUF surface area divided by the deployment time. 



```{r loq, echo=FALSE}
##################################################
# loq
##################################################
sampb.list <- sch.blanks[,1]

lab.b <- subset(sch.blanks, grepl("^LB",bid))
field.b <- subset(sch.blanks, !grepl("^LB",bid))
sch.blanks <- sch.blanks[,-1]

bl.log <- sch.blanks
bl.avg <- colMeans(bl.log)
bl.std <- apply(bl.log,2,sd)

bl.ub <- as.data.frame(bl.avg + (2.325*bl.std)) # LOQ method 2

bl.loq <- bl.ub

names(bl.loq) <- 'LOQ'
bl.loq <- signif(bl.loq,digits=2)
loq.m <- bl.loq          # lab and field blank loq

names(loq.m) <- 'LOQ as Mass'
loq.m <- cbind(cong.list,loq.m)
loq.mass <- format_engr(loq.m, sigdig = 3, ambig_0_adj = T)
loq.mass1 <- loq.mass[1:47,]
loq.mass2 <- loq.mass[48:94,]
loq.mass3 <- loq.mass[95:141,]
loq.mass4 <- loq.mass[142:188,]
options(knitr.kable.NA='')
loq.f <- cbind(loq.mass1,loq.mass2,loq.mass3,loq.mass4)
knitr::kable(loq.f, row.names = FALSE,  format.args = list(scientific = TRUE),caption='Calculated LOQ as mass (ng) for each congener.', escape = F, valign='t', align='cc', booktabs=TRUE)  %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("HOLD_position", "scale_down"))

```

\newpage

## Surrogate Standard Recoveries

We use ten, 13C PCBs as surrogate standards: 13C 3, 5, 15, 28, 52, 118, 153, 180, 194, 209. Known masses of surrogate standards are spiked into samples to allow for correction due to analytical losses during the extraction process. This also accounts for variability in each sample.

```{r echo=FALSE}


ss.rcv.m <- merge.data.frame(ss.rcv,samp.type)
ss.samp <- ss.rcv.m[ss.rcv.m$materials != 'Blank',1:11] 
ss.samp[,2:ncol(ss.samp)] <- ss.samp[,2:ncol(ss.samp)]*100
ss.samp[,2:ncol(ss.samp)] <- round(ss.samp[,2:ncol(ss.samp)],0)

ss.blank <- ss.rcv.m[ss.rcv.m$materials == 'Blank',1:11]
ss.blank[,2:ncol(ss.blank)] <- ss.blank[,2:ncol(ss.blank)]*100
ss.blank[,2:ncol(ss.blank)] <-round(ss.blank[,2:ncol(ss.blank)],0)

rownames(ss.samp)<-NULL
rownames(ss.blank)<-NULL

ss.rcv.p <- rbind(ss.samp,ss.blank)
ss.rcv.p <- ss.rcv.p[,-1]


kable(ss.samp, align = 'c', col.names = c('BID','13C 3','13C 5','13C 15','13C 28', '13C 52','13C 118','13C 153','13C 180','13C 194','13C 209'), caption='Surrogate standard recoveries as percentages for samples.', booktabs=TRUE) %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = "HOLD_position")

kable(ss.blank, align = 'c', col.names = c('BID','13C 3','13C 5','13C 15','13C 28', '13C 52','13C 118','13C 153','13C 180','13C 194','13C 209'), caption='Surrogate standard recoveries as percentages for blanks.', booktabs=TRUE) %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = "HOLD_position")


ss.mean <- as.numeric(colMeans(ss.rcv.p))
ss.mean <-round(ss.mean,0)
ss.std <- as.numeric(apply(ss.rcv.p,2,sd))
ss.std <- round(ss.std,0)
ss.stat <- paste(ss.mean,'\u00b1',ss.std,sep = ' ')
ss.cong <- c('13C 3','13C 15','13C 31', '13C 52','13C 118','13C 153','13C 180','13C 194','13C 206','13C 209')
ss.stat <- as.data.frame(cbind(ss.cong,ss.stat))

kable(ss.stat, align = 'c', col.names = c('PCB','Percent Recovery'), caption='Average surrogate standard recoveries as percentages by congener.', booktabs=TRUE, escape=F) %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = "HOLD_position") %>% 
  column_spec(1,width = '2.5in') %>%
  column_spec(2,width = '2.5in')


```

\newpage

# References


Bannavti, M. K.; Jahnke, J. C.; Marek, R. F.; Just, C. L.; Hornbuckle, K. C., Room-to-Room Variability of Airborne Polychlorinated Biphenyls in Schools and the Application of Air Sampling for Targeted Source Evaluation. Environmental Science & Technology 2021, 55, (14), 9460-9468. 10.1021/acs.est.0c08149

Frame, G. M.; Cochran, J. W.; Bøwadt, S. S., Complete PCB congener distributions for 17 aroclor mixtures determined by 3 HRGC systems optimized for comprehensive, quantitative, congener-specific analysis. J. High Resol. Chromatogr., 19: 657-668. 10.1002/jhrc.1240191202

Herkert, N. J.; Jahnke, J. C.; Hornbuckle, K. C., Emissions of Tetrachlorobiphenyls (PCBs 47, 51, and 68) from Polymer Resin on Kitchen Cabinets as a Non-Aroclor Source to Residential Air. Environmental Science & Technology 2018, 52, (9), 5154-5160. 10.1021/acs.est.8b00966

Herkert, N. J.; Hornbuckle, K. C., Effects of room airflow on accurate determination of PUF-PAS sampling rates in the indoor environment. Environ Sci-Proc Imp 2018, 20, (5), 757-766. 10.1039/c8em00082d

Hua, J. B. X.; Marek, R. F.; Hornbuckle, K. C., Polyurethane Foam Emission Samplers to Identify Sources of Airborne Polychlorinated Biphenyls from Glass-Block Windows and Other Room Surfaces in a Vermont School. Environmental Science & Technology 2023, 57, (38), 14310-14318. 10.1021/acs.est.3c05195

Marek, R. F.; Thorne, P. S.; Herkert, N. J.; Awad, A. M.; Hornbuckle, K. C., Airborne PCBs and OHPCBs Inside and Outside Urban and Rural U.S. Schools. Environmental Science & Technology  2017,  51 (14), 7853-7860. 10.1021/acs.est.7b01910
